analysis_result: Welch t-test: t=0.0789, p=0.9375. MPNST n=18 mean TMB=2.8167 muts/Mb (median=3.3500); Benign/atypical NF n=36 mean TMB=2.7657 muts/Mb (median=3.3833).
denominator_used: N total with WES = 54; MPNST group n=18 (Tumor Type in ['MPNST', 'Low-Grade MPNST']); Benign/atypical n=36 (Tumor Type in ['Neurofibroma', 'Cutaneous_NF', 'Atypical_NF']); TMB = nonsynonymous PASS mutations / 30 Mb
assumptions_made: Nonsynonymous defined as standard MAF classes (Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Nonstop_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Splice_Site, Translation_Start_Site); Filter: FILTER==PASS (all 7457 MAF rows were PASS); Denominator: 30 Mb; MPNST group = MPNST + Low-Grade MPNST; Benign/atypical NF = Neurofibroma + Cutaneous_NF + Atypical_NF; Clinical 'WES label' matched to MAF 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode' via 'Tumor_' + WES label (e.g., WES label '1083' -> 'Tumor_1083'); Samples with WES but no MAF rows imputed as 0 nonsynonymous mutations; Welch's t-test via scipy.stats.ttest_ind(equal_var=False).
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- Clinical (MOESM4): 108 samples x 15 columns; columns include Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, WES, WES label
- MAF (MOESM5): 7457 rows x 123 columns; all rows have FILTER=='PASS'

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- Filter clinical to WES=='X': 54 samples
- MPNST (MPNST + Low-Grade MPNST): n=18
- Benign/atypical NF (Neurofibroma + Cutaneous_NF + Atypical_NF): n=36

C. VARIABLE CODING
- TMB = count of nonsynonymous PASS mutations per sample / 30 Mb
- Nonsynonymous: Missense, Nonsense, Nonstop, Frame_Shift_(Del|Ins), In_Frame_(Del|Ins), Splice_Site, Translation_Start_Site
- FILTER == 'PASS' (all MAF rows)

D. COMPUTATION
- MAF PASS rows: 7457
- MAF PASS + nonsynonymous rows: 5067
- Mapped clinical 'WES label' to MAF 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode' via prefix 'Tumor_'
- Samples with WES but zero nonsynonymous mutations treated as TMB=0
- Welch's two-sample t-test (equal_var=False)

E. RESULT
- MPNST: n=18, mean TMB=2.8167, median TMB=3.3500
- Benign/atypical NF: n=36, mean TMB=2.7657, median TMB=3.3833
- Welch t = 0.0789; p = 0.937487

F. CAVEATS
- Standard definition of nonsynonymous applied
- 30 Mb is a conventional exome callable-space denominator
- Unmatched clinical samples (no MAF rows) kept as TMB=0 (6 samples)
- Welch's t-test assumes approximately normal distributions; distributions may be skewed (consider Mann-Whitney as robustness)
